Secure email that protects your privacy
Keep your conversations private with Proton Mail, an encrypted email service based in Switzerland.
Create a free account
Get Mail for Business
Trusted by over 100 million
users and organizations
4.4+ app rating
Recommended
by the experts
What makes encrypted email better?
Because what's in your email is your business
Most popular email providers, such as Gmail, Outlook, and Yahoo, scan the content of your emails and use your email address to create a detailed profile on you and profit from your data.
Proton Mail's end-to-end encryption and zero-access encryption ensure only you can see your emails. Not even Proton can view the content of your emails and attachments.
Get your encrypted email
No more trackers in your inbox
Email trackers tell senders and advertisers what you read and click on, and can follow you around the web.
Proton Mail protects you from these digital spies and prevents companies from monitoring you.
Block email trackers
Sign up with no phone number
Get a private email account without handing over more personal data than necessary, making it harder for advertisers, data brokers, and other services to track you online.
Create a free account
We may request extra verification in rare situations to prevent misuse.
Ad free, always free
Proton Mail is a free and secure email service that's powered by our community, not surveillance capitalism. Our
free plan
is supported by paid subscriptions that offer extra features and storage.
Highest standards of privacy
Proton is incorporated and headquartered in Switzerland, meaning your data is protected by some of the world's
strictest privacy laws
.
Switch to Proton Mail in one click
With
Easy Switch
, you can quickly migrate from any email provider to Proton Mail. Import your emails, labels, contacts, and calendars to Proton Mail and set up automatic forwarding to receive future emails in your new encrypted inbox.
Open source and independently audited
All Proton services are
open source
and independently audited for security. We also maintain open-source encryption libraries used by millions of people around the world.

The standard for email privacy
From newsrooms, activists, and international organizations to academics, Nobel Prize winners, and movie characters, Proton Mail is the trusted choice for secure and private communication. Join over 100 million people worldwide who believe their online privacy is worth protecting.
Take back control
Your favorite features, plus privacy
Declutter your inbox
See all your email subscriptions in one place, so you can quickly decide what’s worth keeping and instantly unsubscribe from what’s not.
Safe from spam and phishing
PhishGuard blocks known phishing attempts and lets you know if an email is suspicious. Link confirmation ensures you never fall for malicious websites.
Password protection
Set passwords and expiration dates for your emails to securely send confidential information to your contacts, even if they don't have Proton Mail.
Hide-my-email aliases
Create aliases when you sign up for new websites. By hiding your real email address, you can prevent phishing attacks and reduce spam.
Schedule, undo, or snooze emails
Enjoy the ease and security of Proton Mail on all your devices with our open-source apps for web, desktop, Android, and iPhone.
Available on all platforms
Enjoy the ease and security of Proton Mail on all your devices with our open-source apps for web, desktop, Android, and iPhone.
Keep your communications private
Choose an email that is secure and private by default.
Create a free account
An easy alternative
We provide easy-to-use alternatives to Big Tech services and their surveillance business models. With Proton, your data is protected, not exploited.
Join Proton and take control of your data with our encrypted email, calendar, file storage, VPN, and password manager.
Secure your business
In addition to protecting millions of individuals, Proton secures more than 100,000 businesses, including some of the world’s largest public and private organizations.
Learn how Proton can secure your vital business data and help you comply with data protection and privacy laws.
Get Proton Mail for Business
Trusted by 100,000 organizations
Trusted by 100,000 organizations
Swiss-based
Proton Mail FAQ
What is Proton Mail?
View details
Proton Mail is the world’s largest end-to-end encrypted email service. Proton Mail makes sure no one can access your emails by using end-to-end encryption and zero-access encryption. All of Proton Mail’s encryption happens in the background, meaning anyone can use encrypted email, regardless of technical ability.
Millions of journalists, activists, and everyday citizens around the world trust Proton Mail to protect their communications.
Why do I need a private email?
View details
Everyone benefits from switching to a private email service like Proton Mail. Protecting your emails with end-to-end and zero-access encryption helps you take back control of your data and acts as insurance for your online safety in the event of future data breaches or changes in legislation. On a wider scale, using a secure email like Proton Mail helps
protect democracy and free speech
by ensuring you can exercise your right to privacy wherever you are.
How can I get a private email?
View details
You can switch to a private email by
signing up for Proton Mail
for free or by subscribing to one of our paid plans. Once you have created a Proton Mail account, you can transfer your existing contacts and messages to your new secure inbox using our
Easy Switch tool
. You don’t need any technical expertise to start using private email with Proton Mail.
Do you offer a plan for families?
View details
The
Proton Family plan
lets you protect your family’s online privacy. You can add up to five other family members, and get 3 TB of total storage space, with access to all Proton services and features.
Is Proton Mail safer than Gmail?
View details
Yes. Proton Mail is a safer
alternative to Gmail
because when you use Proton Mail, only you can read your emails. In fact, not even Proton can read your emails. This is not true for Gmail, which retains the decryption keys for your data on the same servers where it stores your messages. This means that Gmail can read your messages or hand them over to the authorities if requested. Services like Gmail that do not store your data with zero-access encryption also put you at an increased risk in the event of a data breach. Proton Mail stores your messages with zero-access encryption, which adds an extra layer of resiliency against data breaches.
Is Proton Mail free?
View details
Proton Mail will always offer a free secure email plan that provides all the basic functionality you expect from your inbox with the same level of security as our paid plans. If you upgrade to one of our paid plans, you will receive advanced email features, such as the ability to add more than one email address, extra storage, custom email domains, and unlimited messages. You will also be supporting our mission to build a better internet with privacy as the default.
Privacy by default

Proton Mail FAQ
What is Proton Mail?
View details
Proton Mail is the world’s largest end-to-end encrypted email service. Proton Mail makes sure no one can access your emails, not even Proton, by using end-to-end encryption and zero-access encryption. Proton Mail is one of the services provided by Proton. We’ve also created
Proton VPN
(new window)
,
Proton Drive
,
Proton Pass
and
Proton Calendar
.
When you send an end-to-end encrypted email with Proton Mail, your message is encrypted on your device and can only be decrypted by your recipient with the corresponding encryption key. With Proton Mail, all the encryption and decryption happens in the background, meaning anyone can use encrypted email, regardless of technical ability.
Is Proton Mail free?
View details
Proton Mail will always offer a free encrypted email plan that provides secure email functionality with basic features. Our free email accounts offer the same level of encryption as any of our paid plans.
If you upgrade to one of our paid plans, you will receive advanced email features, such as additional email addresses, extra storage, custom email domains, and unlimited messages. You will also be supporting our mission to build a better internet where privacy is the default.
Is Proton Mail safe?
View details
We ensure that Proton Mail is as safe as possible by using end-to-end encryption and zero access encryption, meaning that no one — not even Proton — can read the contents of your emails.
Proton Mail data is also protected by strong Swiss privacy laws, and we can only respond to official requests from Swiss authorities. All Proton Mail apps are open source, so anyone can view their code, and we commission independent third-party experts to audit our code to ensure they are safe.
Can I create a free email account?
View details
Yes, we will always offer a free email plan for anyone who needs it. Unlike other providers, you can create a free email account with Proton Mail without sacrificing your privacy. Proton Mail Free plans are funded by those who choose to support us by subscribing to one of our paid plans.
To create your free email account with Proton Mail, you can click
Get Proton for free
in the table above, or go directly to our
signup page
. The Proton Mail Free plan comes with 500 MB of Mail storage, which you can increase up to 1 GB by completing the
Get started with Proton
checklist.

Proton Mail
Proton Calendar
Proton Drive
Proton VPN
(new window)
Proton Pass
Get Proton Mail
Sign in
Open menu
Support for Proton Mail
Find answers to your questions about Proton Mail. How can we help you?
Search
Proton Mail categories
We can help you with every step of using Proton Mail, from setting up your account to sending your first email.
Getting started
Using Proton Mail
Contacts
Custom email domain
Addresses and identities
Proton Mail Bridge
Frequently asked questions
How secure is Proton Mail?
View details
The security of our users is one of our top priorities. Here's how we make Proton Mail secure:
End-to-end encryption:
Messages between Proton Mail users are transmitted and stored on our secure servers in an encrypted format. Because your data is encrypted at all steps, the risk of message interception is largely eliminated.
Zero access to your data:
Only you have the key to decrypt your messages and files stored on our secure servers. Your data isn't accessible to anyone else — not even us. As we don't have the key to access your data, we can't hand it over to third parties.
Password-protected Emails:
Messages between Proton Mail users are end-to-end encrypted automatically. But you can also send end-to-end encrypted messages to people on other email providers using Password-protected Emails or standard PGP encryption.
Open source:
All our apps are open source and independently audited, so anyone can check our code.
Strong physical security:
We've invested heavily in owning and controlling our own server hardware. Our data centers are located at highly secure sites that require biometric access.
Is Proton Mail free? What kind of subscription plans do you offer?
View details
We created Proton Mail to make encrypted email easily accessible to everyone around the world. We know that some of the people who need our service the most will not be able to pay. That's why we offer Proton Mail Free — our free email plan that offers the same level of security and ease of use as our paid plans.
If you'd like more storage, unlimited folders, labels, and custom filters, custom email domains, and more, consider
upgrading to a paid plan
. You'll not only get advanced features. You'll also be supporting our drive to make online privacy and security accessible to everyone.
What is encryption, and what data is encrypted by Proton?
View details
Encryption is a process of converting messages or information in such a way that only authorized parties can read it. Using complex algorithms, a message is transformed into an illegible string of characters. The only way to transform the message into its original format is to use a unique encryption key.
When you send an email to other Proton Mail users or when you receive a message from another Proton Mail user, the message body and the attachments are fully and automatically encrypted at every step. This is called
end-to-end encryption
.
All the messages and attachments you receive in your Proton Mail mailbox are also stored with
zero-access encryption
, including messages from other email providers. Only you have the key to access your messages and files on our secure servers. That means your data isn't accessible to anyone else — not even us.
You can also send end-to-end encrypted messages to people on other email providers using
Password-protected Emails
.
Otherwise, they will be protected with TLS encryption if the non-Proton Mail server supports it.
What's the difference between @proton.me and @protonmail addresses? What email domains can I use to create addresses?
View details
Proton has four different email domains that you can use to create email addresses:
@proton.me
is the default domain for Proton Mail. You'll be asked to create a @proton.me address (yourusername@proton.me) when you sign up.
@protonmail.com
is an alternative domain that you can choose when you sign up or use to create additional addresses (yourusername@protonmail.com).
@pm.me
is our short domain. You can
activate your short email address
(yourusername@pm.me) after you've signed up, if your username is available.
@protonmail.ch
is our original Proton Mail domain. If you joined Proton before 2016, you have an additional free @protonmail.ch address (yourusername@protonmail.ch).
To activate your @proton.me address, log in to your account (
account.proton.me
) and click
Settings → Go to settings → Identity and addresses
.
If you have a paid Proton plan, you can create additional addresses (aliases) using any Proton domain above or your own
custom domain
(@yourdomain.com).
Learn more about types of addresses and aliases
You can also set any address as your default sending address.
Learn how to update your default sending address
.
What is Proton Mail Bridge, and how do I use it with desktop email clients?
View details
Proton Mail Bridge is an app for Windows, macOS, and Linux. It allows you to use your Proton Mail account with third-party email clients such as Outlook, Thunderbird, and Apple Mail while
keeping your mail secure
with Proton Mail’s end-to-end encryption.
Proton Mail Bridge is available for all paid subscribers. To use it,
install the Proton Mail Bridge app
, then
configure your favorite email client
for it.
How do I use a custom domain?
View details
If you have a paid Proton Mail plan, you can use a custom domain to send and receive emails (for example, using the email address
yourname@yourdomain.com
). The number of custom domains you can use depends on your Proton Mail plan.
You can purchase custom domains from third-party registrars, such as GoDaddy, Cloudflare, or Namecheap. To
use a custom domain
with your Proton Mail account:
1. Add your domain to your Proton Mail account
2. Verify your domain so that Proton knows it is genuinely yours
3. Configure your domain registrar’s DNS records so that Proton Mail handles the emails sent and received using that domain
Additional steps are required if you have a
Proton for Business
plan and wish to
add users to your organization
so that they can use your custom domain.
Support for all Proton services

analysis.name: Proton Mail
analysis.tagline: Secure email that protects your privacy
analysis.value_proposition: End-to-end encrypted email headquartered in Switzerland where only you can read your messages — not even Proton.
analysis.ideal_customer: Privacy-conscious individuals, journalists, activists, and businesses needing legally-protected, zero-knowledge encrypted email

features.name: end-to-end encryption
features.description: Automatic E2E encryption for all Proton-to-Proton emails; messages unreadable by Proton or any third party
features.evidence_quote: Messages between Proton Mail users are transmitted and stored on our secure servers in an encrypted format.

features.name: zero-access encryption
features.description: All stored messages encrypted at rest so Proton itself cannot decrypt or access any content
features.evidence_quote: Only you have the key to decrypt your messages and files stored on our secure servers.

features.name: password-protected emails
features.description: Send E2E encrypted messages to non-Proton users by setting a shared password for decryption
features.evidence_quote: Sending to non-Proton Mail users requires setting a password prior to sending.

features.name: pgp encryption support
features.description: Standard OpenPGP encryption for interoperability with non-Proton encrypted email users
features.evidence_quote: you can also send end-to-end encrypted messages to people on other email providers using...standard PGP encryption.

features.name: tls encryption fallback
features.description: TLS used as transport encryption when sending to external servers that support it
features.evidence_quote: they will be protected with TLS encryption if the non-Proton Mail server supports it.

features.name: attachment encryption
features.description: Email attachments encrypted alongside message body in all Proton-to-Proton communications
features.evidence_quote: the message body and the attachments are fully and automatically encrypted at every step.

features.name: email tracker blocking
features.description: Blocks tracking pixels and spy scripts that reveal open/click behavior to senders and advertisers
features.evidence_quote: Proton Mail protects you from these digital spies and prevents companies from monitoring you.

features.name: phishguard
features.description: Detects and blocks known phishing attempts and flags suspicious incoming emails
features.evidence_quote: PhishGuard blocks known phishing attempts and lets you know if an email is suspicious.

features.name: link confirmation
features.description: Prompts users to confirm external links before navigating to prevent malicious redirect attacks
features.evidence_quote: Link confirmation ensures you never fall for malicious websites.

features.name: hide-my-email aliases
features.description: Disposable random email aliases to protect real address; 10 on free/Mail Plus, unlimited on Unlimited
features.evidence_quote: Hide-my-email aliases let you create unique, random email addresses on the fly to use with apps, websites, and more.

features.name: custom email domains
features.description: Use own domain with Proton Mail; 1 domain on Mail Plus, 3 on Unlimited, none on Free
features.evidence_quote: Use your own custom email domain addresses, e.g., you@yourname.com

features.name: multiple email addresses
features.description: Additional encrypted email addresses per account; 1 on free, 10 on Mail Plus, 15 on Unlimited
features.evidence_quote: Email addresses... 1 [free] 10 [Mail Plus] 15 [Unlimited]

features.name: multiple proton email domains
features.description: Four Proton domains available for addresses: @proton.me, @protonmail.com, @protonmail.ch, @pm.me
features.evidence_quote: Proton has four different email domains that you can use to create email addresses: @proton.me @protonmail.com @pm.me @protonmail.ch

features.name: short @pm.me address
features.description: Activate a short yourname@pm.me alias for easy sharing; available on paid plans
features.evidence_quote: Activate an extra and free email alias yourname@pm.me, easy to remember and share.

features.name: unlimited folders and labels
features.description: Unlimited inbox organization via folders and label tags on paid plans; capped at 3 each on free
features.evidence_quote: Folders... 3 [free] Unlimited [paid] | Labels... 3 [free] Unlimited [paid]

features.name: custom filters
features.description: Automated email routing rules; 1 on free, unlimited on paid plans
features.evidence_quote: Custom filters... 1 [free] Unlimited [paid]

features.name: html signatures
features.description: Rich HTML email signatures with logos, images, social media icons, and links; all tiers
features.evidence_quote: Personalize your email signature with logos, images, social media icons, links to your contact details, and more.

features.name: desktop app
features.description: Native desktop application for Windows, Mac, Linux; 14-day trial on free, included on paid
features.evidence_quote: Desktop app... 14-day free trial [free] Included [paid]

features.name: proton mail bridge
features.description: IMAP/SMTP bridge enabling use of Proton Mail with third-party desktop clients like Outlook and Thunderbird
features.evidence_quote: Bridge - Use Proton Mail with your desktop email client.

features.name: easy switch migration
features.description: Import emails, labels, contacts, and calendars from any provider with automatic forwarding setup
features.evidence_quote: With Easy Switch, you can quickly migrate from any email provider to Proton Mail.

features.name: encrypted contacts
features.description: Contacts stored with zero-access encryption including phone, address, birthday, and notes
features.evidence_quote: Securely store your contacts' details... Zero-access encryption ensures even Proton can't access them.

features.name: contact groups
features.description: Group contacts to send emails to multiple recipients simultaneously
features.evidence_quote: Contact groups - Send em[ails to groups]

features.name: proton sentinel program
features.description: Advanced threat protection for high-risk accounts with specialist security support; paid only
features.evidence_quote: Provides the highest level of account security protection and specialist support.

features.name: emergency access
features.description: Grant trusted contacts time-delayed access to account in emergencies; paid plans only
features.evidence_quote: Grant permission to trusted contacts to securely access your Proton account in an emergency, after a set period of time.

features.name: dark web monitoring
features.description: Scans dark web for breached Proton email addresses and sends Security Center alerts with mitigations
features.evidence_quote: Dark Web Monitoring scans hidden parts of the internet for Proton Mail email addresses that have ended up in illegal data markets.

features.name: schedule email
features.description: Schedule emails to be sent at a future date and time
features.evidence_quote: Schedule, undo, or snooze emails

features.name: undo send
features.description: Cancel and recall a sent email within a short post-send window
features.evidence_quote: Schedule, undo, or snooze emails

features.name: snooze emails
features.description: Temporarily hide emails and resurface them at a user-chosen future time
features.evidence_quote: Schedule, undo, or snooze emails

features.name: subscription management
features.description: View all email subscriptions in one place with one-click unsubscribe
features.evidence_quote: See all your email subscriptions in one place, so you can quickly decide what's worth keeping and instantly unsubscribe.

features.name: no phone number signup
features.description: Account creation without mandatory phone number to minimize personal data exposure
features.evidence_quote: Get a private email account without handing over more personal data than necessary.

features.name: open source codebase
features.description: All Proton apps are publicly available on GitHub for community inspection
features.evidence_quote: All Proton services are open source and independently audited for security.

features.name: independent security audits
features.description: Regular third-party security audits of all Proton apps and services
features.evidence_quote: All Proton services are open source and independently audited for security.

features.name: swiss legal jurisdiction
features.description: Data protected by Swiss privacy law, legally preventing mass surveillance compliance
features.evidence_quote: Proton is incorporated and headquartered in Switzerland, meaning your data is protected by some of the world's strictest privacy laws.

features.name: physical server ownership
features.description: Proton owns and controls its own server hardware in biometric-secured data centers
features.evidence_quote: We've invested heavily in owning and controlling our own server hardware. Our data centers...require biometric access.

features.name: ad-free interface
features.description: No advertising; entirely funded by paid subscriptions rather than data monetization
features.evidence_quote: Proton Mail is a free and secure email service that's powered by our community, not surveillance capitalism.

features.name: bundled vpn
features.description: Proton VPN included with Unlimited plan; 10 connections, 20,000+ servers in 140+ countries
features.evidence_quote: Browse privately and access blocked content. Enjoy up to 10 VPN connections and all VPN Plus features.

features.name: bundled password manager
features.description: Proton Pass encrypted password manager bundled with Unlimited; 2FA codes, multi-vault, cross-device sync
features.evidence_quote: Secure your passwords with our encrypted password manager. Includes support for 2FA codes, sync across devices, multiple vaults.

features.name: bundled cloud storage
features.description: Proton Drive encrypted cloud storage for photos and documents bundled with Unlimited plan
features.evidence_quote: Store all your data safely. Enjoy up to 500 GB of storage and all Drive Plus features.

features.name: mobile apps
features.description: Native open-source iOS and Android applications
features.evidence_quote: Enjoy the ease and security of Proton Mail on all your devices with our open-source apps for web, desktop, Android, and iPhone.

features.name: attachment size limit
features.description: 25 MB attachment size cap applied uniformly across all plan tiers
features.evidence_quote: Attachments size... 25 MB [all tiers]

features.name: daily message limit
features.description: Free plan capped at 150 messages per day; paid plans have unlimited sending
features.evidence_quote: Messages per day... 150 [free] Unlimited [paid]

features.name: proton calendar integration
features.description: Encrypted calendar product tightly integrated into the Proton ecosystem
features.evidence_quote: Proton Calendar - Organize your schedule privately.

features.name: two-factor authentication
features.description: 2FA account protection via Proton Authenticator app
features.evidence_quote: Proton Authenticator - Secure your online accounts with 2FA across devices.

features.name: 30-day money-back guarantee
features.description: Full refund available within 30 days for any paid subscription
features.evidence_quote: 30-day money-back guarantee

integrations: Lumo AI (privacy-first AI assistant — Proton ecosystem)
differentiators: End-to-end encryption enabled by default for all users including free tier
Zero-access encryption — Proton cannot read, hand over, or be compelled to produce email content
Swiss legal jurisdiction — GDPR+ protections, outside US/EU surveillance frameworks
Open source and independently audited — full public code transparency
Non-profit model via Proton Foundation — no ad revenue, no data monetization
No phone number required for account creation
Owned physical server infrastructure with biometric access controls
Trusted by over 100 million users including newsrooms, activists, and international organizations

recent_ships.what: Proton Meet — confidential end-to-end encrypted video conferencing
recent_ships.source: Landing page badge 'New': 'Proton Meet New — Talk in private with confidential video conferencing.'

recent_ships.what: Proton Sheets — real-time collaborative encrypted spreadsheets
recent_ships.source: Landing page badge 'New': 'Proton Sheets New — Collaborate on sensitive spreadsheets in real time.'

recent_ships.what: Lumo AI — privacy-first AI assistant where every conversation is confidential
recent_ships.source: Landing page: 'Lumo AI — Ask and ideate with a privacy-first AI assistant.'

praised: Strong privacy posture vs Gmail/Outlook — 'Why ProtonMail is more secure than Gmail' (438↑ HN)
Swiss jurisdiction and zero-access architecture cited as genuine differentiator
Open source transparency and independent audits build trust
complaints: IP address logging: 'Climate activist arrested after ProtonMail provided his IP address' (1285↑, 584c HN)
Removed privacy claim: 'ProtonMail deletes we don't log your IP from website after activist arrested' (681↑ HN)
Law enforcement cooperation: 'Proton Mail Helped FBI Unmask Anonymous Stop Cop City Protester' (435↑ HN)
Apple fees: 'ProtonMail CEO calls Apple's forced in-app purchases Mafia extortion' (681↑ HN)
Data reliability: 'Protonmail can delete the wrong email and nobody cares' (438↑ HN)
Browser security: 'Never connect to ProtonMail using Chrome' (461↑ HN)
Search deindexing: 'Why Did ProtonMail Vanish from Google Search Results?' (426↑ HN)

free_tier.limits: 1 GB storage, 1 email address, 3 folders, 3 labels, 1 custom filter, 150 messages/day, 10 aliases, no custom domain

tiers.name: Proton Free
tiers.headline_inclusions: 1 GB storage, 1 address, E2E encryption, basic email; no credit card required

tiers.name: Mail Plus
tiers.headline_inclusions: 15 GB storage, 10 addresses, 1 custom domain, unlimited folders/labels/filters, priority support

tiers.name: Proton Unlimited
tiers.headline_inclusions: 500 GB, 15 addresses, 3 custom domains, VPN + password manager + Drive, advanced account protection

pricing.notes: Monthly (non-annual) pricing: Mail Plus $4.99/mo, Unlimited $12.99/mo. Annual billing locks in ~20-23% discount. Family and Business plan tabs visible on pricing page but details not in captured source. Proton for Business sold as separate product. 30-day money-back guarantee on all paid plans. Desktop app is a 14-day trial on Free, fully included on paid. 25 MB attachment cap across all tiers. Bulk/programmatic email sending explicitly not supported.

recent_hn.title: Proton Mail now supports post-quantum encryption
You can now use your Gmail account in Proton Mail
Show HN: Prism – Self-hosted notification gateway (Signal, Telegram, WebPush)
Show HN: Prism – Self-hosted notification gateway for privacy
How to enable post-quantum protection in Proton Mail
